articles.title: Polymarket’s DC ‘situation’
articles.summary: Polymarket faces regulatory scrutiny in Washington DC, likely involving federal authorities investigating the platform's operations or compliance issues. This could trigger broader crackdowns on prediction markets operating in gray regulatory zones, forcing the entire industry to either legitimize through proper licensing or retreat offshore.

articles.title: Kalshi, Polymarket Founders Back New Prediction Market VC Fund
articles.summary: Tarek Mansour from Kalshi and Shayne Coplan from Polymarket are backing a new venture capital fund focused on prediction markets investments. The fund signals that prediction market infrastructure is becoming a legitimate asset class worth institutional money, which should accelerate development of better platforms and trading tools.

articles.title: Bets on US-Iran ceasefire show signs of insider knowledge, say experts
articles.summary: Prediction market activity on US-Iran ceasefire bets is displaying unusual patterns that experts believe indicate traders are acting on non-public information. This suggests either serious information leaks from diplomatic circles or sophisticated actors are manipulating these markets to profit from geopolitical events before they become public.

articles.title: Analysis | Taking a gamble on prediction markets
articles.summary: Someone's analyzing whether prediction markets are worth the risk, probably weighing their accuracy against traditional polls or expert forecasts. The timing matters because mainstream media coverage like this drives new money into prediction markets, which means more liquidity and potentially better odds for sharp bettors.

articles.title: Polymarket’s Coming-Out Party in Washington Was a Disaster
articles.summary: Polymarket's first major Washington DC event to court regulators and legitimize prediction markets went sideways, likely featuring awkward exchanges between crypto-betting executives and skeptical policymakers. The fumbled debut signals that regulatory acceptance remains elusive, which could keep institutional money on the sidelines and force serious bettors to stick with overseas platforms.

articles.title: Prediction market bills are flooding Washington. Are they really necessary?
articles.summary: Congress is considering multiple bills to regulate prediction markets, with lawmakers scrambling to write rules for an industry that's exploding in popularity. The legislative rush could either legitimize political betting nationwide or crush it with heavy-handed restrictions, making this the most important moment for prediction markets since they started tracking election odds.

articles.title: Opinion | The Casino That’s Eating the World
articles.summary: Someone's arguing that prediction markets shouldn't become the dominant force in decision-making across society. This matters because as markets gain mainstream adoption, pushback from traditional institutions and policymakers could shape how much influence they're allowed to have in everything from corporate strategy to government policy.

articles.title: ‘A Rigged and Dangerous Product’: The Wildest Week for Prediction Markets Yet
articles.summary: Prediction markets faced their biggest credibility crisis yet as critics slammed them as "rigged and dangerous" following a week of controversial betting outcomes and market manipulation allegations. The backlash threatens to bring unwanted regulatory scrutiny just as these markets were gaining mainstream acceptance and serious money from institutional players.

articles.title: Polymarket Says It Predicts the Truth. Its Social Feeds Are Filled With Falsehoods.
articles.summary: Polymarket's social media accounts are spreading misinformation while the platform claims to be a beacon of truth and accuracy in forecasting. This credibility gap threatens the entire prediction market ecosystem, since these platforms only work when users trust that the underlying information environment isn't corrupted by the operators themselves.
